strings.tag: Hi [name], this is a test string from room004 - csi lab.
Welcome to the Crime Lab, how can I help you?
Click-and-drag any piece of evidence to the correct processing station for analysis. Or click-and-drag the evidence to your P.D.A and it will tell you what to do with it.
Sure, I can tell you about any of them. What do you want to know?
Hair, blood, spit, sweat - this is the place to analyze it. The computer also lets us search DNA samples through CODIS.
That station has all the powders and chemicals you need to process any item for fingerprints. Use the computer to search for prints in IAFIS.
This is the computer we use for database searches - license plates, addresses, anything. Also, if you have any digital evidence, like a computer or a flash drive, this is the place to take it.
What do you want to know?
That table is very useful for analyzing and comparing large pieces of evidence like shoe prints.
This is the station for comparing toolmarks with the tools that make them. First, you'll make an impression of the toolmark, then compare it under the microscope.
How do I process evidence?
Tell me about the processing stations.
Tell me about the DNA station.
Tell me about the fingerprint station.
Tell me about the digital forensics station.
I want to ask about a different station.
Tell me about the light table.
Tell me about the toolmark comparison station.
I'm ready to process evidence now.
What did you learn from the impression evidence?
What was identical between the shoe impressions?
That's a good result, but it's meaningless unless you identify who made both impressions.
That's not right, try again.
Someone was at both the crime scene and Freebird's house.
The person who wore these shoes killed Freebird Reynolds.
There were multiple people at the crime scene.
The class characteristics.
The individual characteristics.
Both the class and individual characteristics.
I'll continue with my investigation.
What did you learn from the impression evidence?
How do you know other shoes can't have the same individual characteristics?
It's interesting, but it doesn't prove he murdered Freebird. You'll need to combine this with other clues.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
Gabriel Tam killed Freebird Reynolds.
Gabriel Tam was present at both the crime scene and Freebird's house.
Gabriel Tam altered his shoes to disguise them.
Some shoes DO have the same individual characteristics.
Individual characteristics are caused by unique wear-and-tear during the life of the shoe.
Gabriel Tam created the individual characteristics to deceive us.
I'll go continue my investigation.
What did you learn from the fingerprint comparison?
How did Maury Wan's fingerprints get into IAFIS?
What technique did you use to develop the fingerprints on the liquor bottle?
Maury Wan's last known address is included in his IAFIS profile. We should go see if he knows anything about this.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
That's not true, try again.
Someone named Maury Wan killed our victim.
Someone named Maury Wan likes to drink this type of liquor.
Someone named Maury Wan left his fingerprints on a bottle found at the crime scene.
Obviously, he must have committed a crime.
He was fingerprinted for a background check.
Every American's fingerprint is in IAFIS.
I brushed the bottle with fingerprint powder.
I used the Superglue Fuming Method.
The fingerprints were naturally visible on the surface of the bottle.
I'll head to his office when I'm done here.
What did you learn from the fingerprint comparison?
When you used the superglue to develop the fingerprints on the hunting knife, what did the superglue react with?
That's a very interesting result. You should see what else you can learn about this hunting knife.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
I don’t think so, try again.
Gabriel Tam murdered Freebird Reynolds.
Gabriel Tam once handled this hunting knife.
Someone planted Gabriel Tam's fingerprints on this knife.
The natural oils and amino acids left behind by Gabriel's skin.
The fingerprint powder I spread on the hunting knife.
I didn't use superglue fuming on the hunting knife.
I'll see what other forensic tests I can run on the hunting knife.
It looks like your toolmark comparison experiment is going well. What did you learn?
What part of the stab wound and hunting knife toolmarks were identical?
Finally, what lab equipment did you use to compare the striations?
Well, you've determined that the hunting knife was the murder weapon. Now you need to link it to a particular suspect.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
That's not true, try again.
Lawrence Garvey murdered Freebird Reynolds.
The hunting knife made the wound that killed Freebird Reynolds.
All knives make identical stab wounds.
The shape of the wound.
The striations (small thin lines) left on the material by the blade.
The class characteristics left on the material by the blade.
A light table.
A comparison microscope.
An electron microscope.
I'll get right on that.
What did you learn from the DNA profile?
What are you going to do next?
I hope Doc Robbins has found something. You'll find him in the morgue.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
I don’t think so, try again.
You can't give up just because you hit a roadblock. There's bound to be other things you can do.
That's not feasible. There's got to be a more realistic solution.
There was a matching DNA profile in CODIS.
There was NOT a matching DNA profile in CODIS.
This is fake DNA; it is impossible not to have a match in CODIS.
There is nothing else to do. I give up.
I'll go see if Doc Robbins has had any luck identifying the victim.
I'll collect DNA profiles from everyone in Las Vegas to find a match.
See you later!
What did you learn from the DNA profiles?
Are you sure? How many of their DNA markers are identical?
If 50% of our victim's DNA markers are identical to Sallie Reynolds, where did the other 50% come from?
You do good work, Rookie. Our victim finally has a name: Freebird Reynolds.
We can't jump to that conclusion, try again.
That's not true, try again.
Sallie Reynolds killed Freebird Reynolds.
Sallie Reynolds is our victim's daughter.
Sallie Reynolds is our victim's mother.
Their DNA is exactly identical.
They have 50 identical markers.
50% of their DNA markers are identical.
The other 50% are completely random.
The other 50% are from the victim's father.
The other 50% are continually changing as a person grows older.
I'll go see if there's more evidence to process.
Are you sure you've done a thorough investigation? What's the first thing you did with Freebird's computer?
Why did you image the hard drive?
What files did you look at on the imaged drive?
Sounds like a good initial digital forensic investigation. I'll have our experts go over it with a fine-tooth comb to make sure we didn't miss anything. What did you learn?
I'll ask Brass to get a warrant for the internet company's records. We'll find out who this "No1FAN" is.
Is that all you did?
That's not true, try again.
I imaged the hard drive.
I erased the hard drive.
I read all her emails.
To protect the lab computers from any computer viruses.
To find any illegal files.
To create a complete and accurate copy of all the digital evidence.
I read her e-mails.
I looked at her recently modified documents.
I looked at her deleted files.
I examined her e-mails, the recently modified documents, and her deleted files.
Freebird and her boyfriend are engaged.
Freebird had a fight with her mother.
Freebird received some strange e-mails from a "No1FAN."
Great. I'll continue my investigation.
That was an ... interesting ... investigation! Why did you have to re-image the drive?
What did those booby-traps do?
Exactly. Working on the imaged drive kept all the original evidence safe and secure from the booby-traps. So what were the booby-traps protecting?
That's good investigating. I'll have our digital experts go over the drive more to make sure we didn't miss anything. Why don't you go continue your investigation?
That's not true, try again.
Gabriel Tam booby-trapped his computer.
The lab computer kept erasing the hard drive.
Gabriel Tam infected the lab computer with a virus.
They erased the lab computer.
They erased our copy of his hard drive.
They erased all the digital evidence we collected.
A picture of Lawrence murdering Freebird.
A picture of Gabriel Tam murdering Freebird.
The "No1FAN" emails that Gabriel Tam sent to Freebird.
I'll go see what Gabriel has to say about this.

There's a better station to use to analyze that evidence.

Try using it somewhere else.
MATCH
HELP
SEARCH
SUBMIT
BEGIN
DONE
START OVER
GET HELP
Tell me more
Use the magnifying glass to identify any unique markings on both shoes.

This is the light table.
Use it to analyze evidence such as shoes and shoeprint impressions.

These seem to be a match. They have many class characteristics in common, including: • shoe size
• manufacturer
• approximate age.

Use the magnifying glass to identify any unique markings on both shoes. Then we can be sure they're identical.

You've identified the individual characteristics for these shoes - small, distinctive wear-and-tear marks unique for each individual shoe.

Unfortunately, these shoes do not have the same individual characteristics. They are NOT a match.

Now that's a good match.

All the individual characteristics - the small, unique marks from normal wear-and-tear - are identical between these impressions. They must have come from the same shoe.
The individual characteristics between the shoe and the shoe print are identical! You made a good match, rookie.
Shoes
Impression
Crime Scene
Freebird's House
Maury Wan's
Lawrence Garvey's
Gabriel Tam's

This is the fingerprint station.
Use it to gather fingerprints from evidence you have collected and to compare fingerprints you have collected.

We'll use the "Superglue Fuming Method" to develop these fingerprints. It can detect even the faintest prints.

First, set up the experiment. It will only take 10 minutes or so to develop the prints.
Place the object in the fuming chamber.
Add Superglue to the warming tray.
Place the warming tray on the heater inside the fuming chamber.
Turn on the heater
Close lid of chamber

Fumes from the evaporating superglue react with amino acids and oils left behind on everything we touch. The reaction produces a white, sticky material, and makes the fingerprint easy to see.

I'll scan this print into the computer for you to analyze.

Print Added

This new print source has been added to the computer for comparison with the prints found during the investigation.
Drag a fingerprint to a "Viewing area" to begin.
Identify as many ridge characteristics in the fingerprint as possible.
Select a second fingerprint for comparison, or search the database.

Class Characteristics

Is this fingerprint a loop, whorl, or arch?
Scanned fingerprints:
Possible matches:
Loop
Whorl
Arch

Class Characteristics of Fingerprints

Class Characteristics of fingerprints are shared by an entire group or type of fingerprints. For example, there are three main classes of fingerprints:

LOOP
The ridges enter and exit the same side of the finger

WHORL
The ridges form a circular or spiral pattern

ARCH
The ridges run from one side of the finger to the other

Individual Characteristics of Fingerprints

Individual Characteristics are unique to specific, individual fingerprints. These are also called ridge characteristics or minutia.

Common Ridge Characteristics
BIFURCATION
ISLAND (SHORT RIDGE)
HOOK (SPUR)
LAKE (ENCLOSURE)
RIDGE ENDING
Bifurcation
Island
Hook
Lake
Ridge Ending

IAFIS

The Integrated Automated Fingerprint Identification System (IAFIS) is the FBI's national fingerprint database. It contains over 470,000,000 fingerprints. On average, it takes about two hours to search the entire database.

Match detected!

Name: Maury Wan
Occupation: Publicity Agent
Address: 1589 Avenue A, Las Vegas

Fingerprint Source: Pre-employment Background Check

Match detected!

Name: Lawrence Garvey


Fingerprint Source: Collected during investigation

Match detected!

Name: Gabriel Tam


Fingerprint Source: Collected during investigation
You don't have the right tools to use this machine. You need the wound impression, impression gel, and to use at least one weapon on this station to proceed.

To compare this weapon with the impression left in our victim's cartilage, we will need to make a cast of its cut.

We'll be using a material similar in consistency to rib cartilage. I'll walk you through the procedure.
Cut the cartilage-like material with the weapon.
Use the impression gel on the edges of the cut.
Allow the casting gel to harden
Remove the hardened cast for analysis.
Waiting for gel to harden.
Not bad. Now we can analyze the cast under the comparison microscope. It will have the same individual characteristics as the knife that made the cut if it is the murder weapon.
Cartilage Cast
Hunting Knife Cast
Sword Cast
You have some experience in processing DNA during your Rookie Training, so I'll handle that part of it. I'll send the DNA profile to the computer when I'm done.
Processing DNA ...

DNA Profiling

A DNA profile contains 13 unique genetic markers (known DNA sequences). No two individuals (except identical twins) have the same exact markers.

CODIS

The Combined DNA Index System (CODIS) is a database of over 4 million DNA profiles. The profiles come from convicted felons, evidence, and missing persons.
Possible parental match detected! Initializing Familial comparison program...

Family DNA Comparisons

Everyone inherits 2 copies of each DNA marker - one from the mother, one from the father. Each peak has a number code. You can tell which copies you've inherited by looking at the numbers coded underneath the peaks.
Click on the number of the peak that the son inherited.

Family DNA Comparisons

Can you determine which DNA markers the son inherited from both his parents?

You are now ready to begin the family DNA comparison.

DIRECTIONS
For each marker in the DNA profiles, click only the marker numbers shared by both the mother and the victim. When you think you have found all the identical marker numbers, click "SUBMIT"

50% DNA Match Confirmed!

DNA samples are from a parent and child.
Victim DNA Profile:
Possible Matches:
Victim's Bone Sample
Sallie Reynolds
SEARCH CODIS
CONFIRM MATCH
RUN FAMILY COMPARISON
Mother's DNA
Father's DNA
Son's DNA
Drag DNA sample here to view.
No sample loaded in the viewing area.
DNA samples are NOT identical.
No familial match detected.
I couldn't find any DNA on that piece of evidence.
Drag the Victim’s DNA to the left field
Drag a possible match to the right field to compare them
Now drag Sallie Reynold's DNA to the right field to compare them
Click only the marker numbers shared by both the mother and the victim.

DIGITAL FORENSICS

Analyzing digital evidence consists of TWO PARTS:

• Create an EXACT COPY of all digital evidence.
• ANALYZE that evidence for interesting activity.

Freebird's computer has already been connected to the lab computers, so we are ready to begin.
Critical Steps in a Digital Forensics Investigation:
Make a copy of the suspect's hard drive.
Examine all e-mail correspondence.

Examine any recently
altered files.
Examine all deleted files.
Ready to begin Drive Imaging

"Imaging" the Hard Drive

To preserve the evidence, investigators NEVER work directly on the suspect's hard drive. Instead, they create a copy, or IMAGE of all the digital information on the drive. It is vital that the image is both ACCURATE and COMPLETE.
Imaging Progress:
BEGIN EXAMINATION
Recycle Bin
My E-mail
My Recent Documents
My Pics
Music Maker
My E-mail Client
Inbox
Sent
Trash
Lawrence
Sallie Reynolds
No1FAN
No1FAN
Where are you?
Call Mom!
Can't wait to see you!
Your #1 Fan - ALWAYS

From: Lawrence Garvey
To: Freebird Reynolds

Where are you?
I'm sorry about what I said.
We really need to talk.
Call me!!!!!!!

From: Sallie Reynolds
To: Freebird Reynolds

Loved the concert, honey - you were amazing!
We need to talk about dinner next week, k?
I want to catch up with you!

From: No1FAN
To: Freebird Reynolds

I'm lookin 4ward 2 your concert 2nite, Frebird! I can't wait 2 finalE Meet U in person! I'll b lookin 4 U after the concert!

From: No1FAN
To: Freebird Reynolds

Freebird, U don't knO me, but U will soon. I tink you're absolUtly amazing, & 1 day I hope 2 tell U n person how much I rly, rly luv U!
Mom, Lawrence
Concert

From: Freebird Reynolds
To: Sallie Reynolds, Lawrence

Hey both of you! I've arranged for your tickets to the concert.

Lawrence, can you and I do dinner afterwards? We need to talk.
THIS FOLDER IS EMPTY
My Recent Documents
Document Editor
Resignation Letter
Super Secret Diary Code

Mr. Wan,

This letter is to inform you I wish to end our contract together. I have decided to pursue

(Note to self: finish later after the concert)

A=J
B=E
C=S
D=M
E=A
F=O
G=W
H=!
Recycle Bin
This folder is empty.
Music Maker
My Pictures
Postage E-mail
Recent Files
Trash
DO NOT OPEN
Postage E-mail Client
Inbox
Sent
Trash
THIS FOLDER IS EMPTY
THIS FOLDER IS EMPTY
THIS FOLDER IS EMPTY
Recent Documents
DO NOT OPEN folder

H4CK1NG 4CT1V1TY D3T3C3T3D!!!!!!

N0w 3r4s1ng H4rd Dr1v3

Y0U'V3 B33N
PWN3D!!!!

Run into problems?

Now you see why we always image the drive first. That booby-trap only erased our copy. The actual evidence is still intact.

You'll have to create a new image of the drive to continue. Watch out for more booby-traps.
Trash

4r3 y0u sur3?

My tr4sh 1s pr0t3ct3d!!

Y0u w1ll h4v3 t0 34rn th3 r1ght t0 l00k thr0ugh @ th1s f0ld3r!

It looks like his deleted files are well protected. I wonder what he's hiding?

Tell you what. I can give you a hand to get past this, or you can start over and do it on your own.

Which do you choose?

You'll have to re-image the hard drive again. That trap completely erased our copy.

Keep an eye out - there may be more traps or defenses.

Good thing this trap erased only our copy of the drive. No evidence was harmed.

I'll re-image the drive and get to work. Watch and learn, rookie.

That should do it.

Now let's see what files he was protecting.
USE ARROW KEYS TO MOVE AND SPACE BAR TO SHOOT.
Saved E-mails
My Pics
Postage E-mail (Deleted E-mails)
Freebird Reynolds
Freebird Reynolds
Can't wait to see you!
Your #1 Fan - ALWAYS

From: No1FAN
To: Freebird Reynolds

I'm lookin 4ward 2 your concert 2nite, Frebird! I can't wait 2 finalE Meet U in person! I'll b lookin 4 U after the concert!

From: No1FAN
To: Freebird Reynolds

Freebird, U don't knO me, but U will soon. I tink you're absolUtly amazing, & 1 day I hope 2 tell U n person how much I rly, rly luv U!
This station is for analyzing digital evidence - computer, flash drives, cell phones, etc.
